title: Palantir Developers: Learn to build in Palantir Foundry

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core platform designed to be intuitive and accessible for both technical and non-technical users across an organization

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application that simplifies app building for developers and operational users

extractedEntities.name: Quiver
extractedEntities.description: Foundry tool that helps developers overlay millions of sensor data points in seconds for geospatial and time series analysis

extractedEntities.name: Palantir Developers Channel
extractedEntities.description: Video channel launched as a hub for developer support, tutorials, and walkthroughs of Foundry features

extractedEntities.name: Stack Overflow
extractedEntities.description: External Q&A platform adopted by Palantir for developer engagement and community support for Foundry

extractedEntities.name: Developer Community Engagement
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of engaging with developers through Stack Overflow, video tutorials, and reference projects to accelerate platform adoption

extractedEntities.name: Foundry Reference Project
extractedEntities.description: Template project used in developer tutorials and walkthroughs to demonstrate Foundry capabilities

keyInsights: Palantir aims to make Foundry accessible beyond data scientists and engineers to non-technical roles like sales, product development, and recruiting
Stack Overflow became an organic community hub for Foundry developer questions, which Palantir later formalized as a core engagement channel
Workshop and Quiver were co-created with customer developers, illustrating Palantir's embedded development model where customers shape product features

title: Palantir Foundry: Supercharge Your BI & Analytics

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Platform providing best-in-class data integration and ontology capabilities that enrich and extend existing BI and analytics tooling

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Common data layer sitting on top of integrated digital artifacts, formulating a digital twin of real-world business entities with multi-modal storage support

extractedEntities.name: Digital Twin
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of using the Ontology to create a digital representation of real-world things that constitute a business, such as plants, customers, and transactions

extractedEntities.name: Decision Orchestration
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where the Ontology orchestrates the flow of data, models, and actions across heterogeneous tooling and systems for continuous learning

extractedEntities.name: Quiver
extractedEntities.description: Analytical suite allowing users to plot, transform, and analyze massive-scale time series data and objects

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application enabling non-technical users to build interactive decision-making applications

extractedEntities.name: Foundry Connectors
extractedEntities.description: Integration mechanisms including REST APIs, ODBC/JDBC drivers, Python/R SDKs, and out-of-the-box connectors for Power BI and Tableau

extractedEntities.name: Unified Security and Governance
extractedEntities.description: Foundry's integrated security, lineage, and governance model from source to application with per-artifact permissions and retention policies

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology element that captures the kinetics between objects and orchestrates real-world change through enterprise systems

extractedEntities.name: Connected Operations
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of creating full-fidelity feedback loops between data, analytics, and operational workflows rather than a linear assembly-line approach

keyInsights: Foundry's Ontology acts as a common data layer with multi-modal storage supporting diverse functionality from search to time series analysis to geospatial visualization
Foundry provides automatic data lineage tracing from raw data all the way to applications, eliminating manual pipeline documentation
The Ontology enables read-write capabilities in workflows, creating feedback loops for continuous learning rather than one-directional data consumption
Security governance in Foundry can be applied at multiple granularities: individual datasets, rows within datasets, folders, projects, and more
Foundry integrates with existing BI tools (Power BI, Tableau) through REST APIs, ODBC/JDBC drivers, and language-specific SDKs

title: The Ontology: Operating at optimum complexity — as simply as possible

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Foundational element of the modern software stack that transforms digital assets into a dynamic, actionable foundation powering intelligent operations

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology elements that digitally represent real-world entities, relationships, and events constituting a business

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology elements representing connections between real-world entities, events, and processes

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology elements capturing kinetics between objects and orchestrating real-world change through enterprise systems

extractedEntities.name: Optimum Complexity
extractedEntities.description: Principle that technology should empower businesses to understand, achieve, and sustain the right level of complexity as simply as possible

extractedEntities.name: Digital Twin
extractedEntities.description: Concept of creating a digital representation of business entities, distinct from traditional process models or capability maps by focusing on stable elements

extractedEntities.name: Feedback Loop
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where actions triggered by insights feed information back into the system, capturing on-the-ground truth from insight to impact

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir platform whose key differentiator is the Ontology, enabling organizations to connect data, analytics, and operational systems

keyInsights: The Ontology is positioned as an entirely new category of software, not merely a data model or integration layer
Traditional digital twin models fail because they fix variable elements and miss stable ones; the Ontology inverts this by building around the most stable elements of the business
The three core Ontology primitives are Objects (entities), Relations (connections), and Actions (kinetics/change orchestration)
Data and analytics are meaningless without connectivity to real-world action — the Ontology's core value proposition is bridging insight to operational impact
Organizations must embrace complexity rather than avoid it, and the Ontology provides the means to manage that complexity at the right level

title: The Ontology: Resilience in crisis

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Software system enabling collaboration across entire organizations that transforms digital assets into a dynamic, actionable representation of the business

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology elements digitally representing real-world entities, relationships, and events such as transformers, substations, customers, and field crews

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology elements representing connections between real-world entities, events, and processes

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology elements capturing kinetics between objects, which can be mapped from existing processes or models

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Hydration
extractedEntities.description: Process of continuously synchronizing data and models into human-centric objects within the Ontology from diverse source systems

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir platform whose key differentiator is the Ontology, enabling organizational resilience in crisis scenarios

extractedEntities.name: Utility Grid Management
extractedEntities.description: Use case where the Ontology models transformers, substations, customers, and field crews for emergency wildfire response

extractedEntities.name: Refugee Relief Operations
extractedEntities.description: Use case where the Ontology powered support for refugee relief across Europe amid the war in Ukraine

extractedEntities.name: Healthcare Waiting List Management
extractedEntities.description: Use case at Chelsea and Westminster Hospital NHS Foundation Trust for elective waiting list management

extractedEntities.name: Energy Optimization
extractedEntities.description: Use case for power utilization optimization in manufacturing and wastewater treatment amid rising energy prices

keyInsights: The Ontology is explicitly not a pre-defined data model but a software system that enables collaboration, analogous to a language with pre-defined rules for constructing complex structures
Ontology 'hydration' is the process of continuously synchronizing data and models from ERP, CRM, IoT, GIS, and other systems into human-centric objects
Enterprise software failed organizations in critical moments because linear approaches (data gathering, golden tables, models, visualization) proved rigid and unadaptable in crisis
The Ontology converts technical artifacts (rows, columns, queries) into business-meaningful objects (transformers, substations, customers) enabling non-technical users to act on data
Crisis scenarios (wildfires, pandemics, wars, supply chain shocks) expose the failure of rigid enterprise software, which the Ontology's adaptable model addresses

title: User-Centered Machine Learning for Visual Search

extractedEntities.name: UCML
extractedEntities.description: User-Centered Machine Learning platform enabling end users to rapidly adapt cutting-edge Computer Vision capabilities to specific missions based on their feedback

extractedEntities.name: UCML for Visual Search
extractedEntities.description: Extension of UCML that allows analysts to detect any object in any image in real-time using foundational visual-language models, without data labeling or model training

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Foundational metadata system that enriches visual search detections with data modalities and object metadata to create a picture of what, where, and when

extractedEntities.name: Visual Search Query Pipeline
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where users indicate pixels via bounding box, optionally provide text prompts, and vision-language models extract features to identify similar pixels across images

extractedEntities.name: Vision-Language Models
extractedEntities.description: Foundational models that extract unique features from user-indicated pixels and text to identify similar objects across image sets

extractedEntities.name: Zero-Shot Object Detection
extractedEntities.description: Capability to generate object detection for any object of interest from as little as one example, without pre-trained models for that object class

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Fusion
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of fusing visual search detections with additional data modalities and foundational metadata from the Ontology for enriched analysis

extractedEntities.name: Conflict Imagery Analysis
extractedEntities.description: Use case where analysts detect new infrastructure like bridges for armored vehicles or communications equipment as they emerge during conflicts

keyInsights: UCML for Visual Search enables zero-shot object detection from as little as one example, eliminating the need for data labeling, model training, or dedicated detectors
Visual search queries can be used interactively, bulk-run on imagery archives, or deployed on image feeds for continuous monitoring and alerting
The Palantir Ontology enriches visual search detections by fusing them with additional data modalities and metadata, creating context about what, where, and when
The system combines pixel-based visual features with optional free-text prompts to leverage vision-language models for more specific detection
The approach addresses the critical military/intelligence need for real-time adaptation to new object types during active conflicts without waiting for traditional ML pipelines

title: Action types - Side effects - Overview - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology element designed to support the full range of decision-making processes within an organization, using rules to define object modifications

extractedEntities.name: Side Effect
extractedEntities.description: Additional capability of action types that enables sending data out of Foundry to integrate with existing organizational processes, triggered after primary action rules execute

extractedEntities.name: Notification
extractedEntities.description: Side effect type that allows flexible configuration of how users should be notified when an action is applied, including email notifications

extractedEntities.name: Webhook
extractedEntities.description: Side effect type enabling connections to systems outside Foundry in a highly flexible way, including REST API calls and ERP system integration

extractedEntities.name: Decision Orchestration
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where Foundry integrates with external source-of-truth systems to support existing business processes through action type side effects

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Platform whose action types support side effects for integration with external systems beyond Ontology edits

keyInsights: Action type side effects are triggered after primary action rules execute, maintaining a clear separation between Ontology modifications and external integrations
The two main side effect types are Notifications (user alerting within Foundry, including email) and Webhooks (external system integration via REST APIs and ERP connections)
The 'decision orchestration' pattern arises when an external system (not Foundry) is the source of truth, and side effects enable Foundry to integrate with and write to those systems
Side effects enable Foundry's Ontology to serve as a coordination layer even when it is not the system of record

title: Object and link types - Structs - Structs and shared properties - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Struct
extractedEntities.description: Composite property type in the Ontology that groups multiple fields into a single structured value, usable by both local and shared property types

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: Reusable property type definition that can be shared across multiple object types, enabling consistent metadata and field definitions across the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Local property type on an object type that can optionally be backed by a shared property type for metadata inheritance

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element whose properties can use struct types backed by shared property definitions

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application for creating, configuring, and managing ontology elements including shared properties and struct types

extractedEntities.name: Struct Field RID
extractedEntities.description: Resource identifier for struct fields that is preserved when converting local struct properties to shared property-backed types, even as metadata is inherited

keyInsights: Struct properties can be used by both local and shared property types, enabling complex composite types to be reused across multiple object types
When promoting a local struct property to a shared property type, struct fields must be re-mapped but retain their original RIDs while inheriting shared metadata
New struct fields added after assigning a shared property type must be added to the shared property type first, then mapped to datasource columns for all local struct properties backed by it
The promotion workflow (local to shared) preserves backward compatibility by keeping original struct field RIDs intact

title: Object and link types - Metadata - Type classes - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Type Class
extractedEntities.description: Additional metadata applied to properties, link types, and action types that defines behavior interpreted by user applications interacting with the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Capability
extractedEntities.description: New configuration mechanism in Ontology Manager replacing type classes, providing a dedicated page for configuring features historically defined as type classes

extractedEntities.name: Hubble Type Class
extractedEntities.description: Category of type classes consumed by Object Explorer that affect property rendering including media URLs, icons, thumbnails, and endorsement status

extractedEntities.name: Timeseries Type Class
extractedEntities.description: Category of type classes consumed by Quiver for time series data visualization and analysis

extractedEntities.name: Analyzer Type Class
extractedEntities.description: Special type class that affects indexing behavior rather than rendering, distinct from other type classes that only provide presentation metadata

extractedEntities.name: Render Hint
extractedEntities.description: Type class metadata that controls how property values are displayed in Object Views, such as media rendering, icon display, and endorsement status

extractedEntities.name: Object Explorer
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application that consumes hubble type classes for rendering object properties in views and search results

extractedEntities.name: Quiver
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application that consumes timeseries type classes for time series data display

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application where type classes are configured and are being migrated to a new Capabilities page

keyInsights: Type classes are being migrated from a string-based Kind/Name system to a dedicated Capabilities page in Ontology Manager, indicating an evolution in ontology metadata management
The analyzer type class is unique among type classes in that it affects indexing behavior rather than just presentation/rendering metadata
Many type classes are deprecated, showing the Ontology system's evolution from tag-based metadata to first-class configuration primitives
Type classes create a contract between ontology definitions and consuming applications (Object Explorer, Quiver), decoupling data modeling from presentation
Hubble type classes control property rendering in Object Views including media URLs, icons, thumbnails, editability, and endorsement status

title: Object and link types - Properties - Required properties - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Required Property
extractedEntities.description: Object type property that must have a value, validated during data indexing and action execution to reject null values and optionally empty arrays

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Attribute of an object type that can be configured as required to enforce data quality constraints

extractedEntities.name: Object Storage V2
extractedEntities.description: Second-generation object storage backend required for required property support, representing the modern storage layer

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element whose properties can be configured with required constraints for data quality enforcement

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology element whose edits are validated against required property constraints at apply time, failing if null or empty values are written

extractedEntities.name: Required Property Validation
extractedEntities.description: Validation occurs during indexing into Object Storage (not during ontology modification), and at action apply time, with specific behavior for empty arrays and multi-datasource scenarios

extractedEntities.name: Multi-Datasource Null Behavior
extractedEntities.description: When an object type has multiple backing datasources, null values may appear in required properties if a record exists in some datasources but is absent from the one supplying the required property

extractedEntities.name: Mandatory Control Property
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of using required properties that allow empty arrays for control properties that must never be null but can legitimately have no items

keyInsights: Required properties are only supported by Object Storage V2, indicating an ongoing migration from legacy storage to newer object storage backends
Validation timing is critical: the ontology modification itself succeeds even if backing data contains nulls; validation only fails during indexing into Object Storage
Actions automatically write empty arrays to mapped-but-unset parameters, which interacts subtly with required property enforcement and the 'allow empty arrays' configuration
Multi-datasource object types can have surprising null values in required properties when a record is missing from the datasource that supplies the required property
The 'allow empty arrays' option enables the mandatory control property pattern where a property must exist but can legitimately be empty

title: Object and link types - Object type groups - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Object Type Group
extractedEntities.description: Classification primitive that helps users search and explore their ontology by labeling and categorizing object types, replacing the legacy tag-based grouping system

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element that can belong to multiple groups for organizational classification

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application where groups are created, managed, searched, and filtered via a dedicated Groups menu in the sidebar

extractedEntities.name: Object Explorer
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application whose home page displays groups to help users discover and navigate object types

extractedEntities.name: Legacy Group Migration
extractedEntities.description: Migration from tag-based legacy groups to first-class ontology primitives as of May 2024, with automatic migration for most groups and manual migration required for partially visible groups

extractedEntities.name: Group Permissions
extractedEntities.description: Access control mechanism where viewing object type groups requires viewer permission on the containing project, with all groups discoverable to any ontology viewer

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Classification
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of using groups as labels on object types to improve searchability, discoverability, and organizational structure of large ontologies

keyInsights: Object type groups replaced the legacy tag-based grouping system as of May 2024, becoming a first-class ontology primitive rather than metadata on object types
Groups are now discoverable to any user who can view the ontology, aligning group visibility with other ontology primitives for transparency in governance
Object types can belong to multiple groups, providing flexible multi-dimensional classification for large ontologies
Legacy groups that were not fully visible to all users required manual migration by ontology owners, with hidden groups stored as type class metadata for backward compatibility
Groups serve both organizational (classification) and operational (search, filtering, Object Explorer home page) purposes

title: Understanding the Palantir Ontology - PVM Blog

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Heart of all Palantir platforms serving as a framework to bring order and structure to complex datasets, integrating diverse data points into a unified system

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Data entity defined in the Ontology with attributes and relationships to other entities

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Attribute of an entity within the Ontology's hierarchical data model

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Relationship definition between data entities within the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Operational element in the Ontology enabling decision-making and action on data

extractedEntities.name: Hierarchical Data Model
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where the Ontology defines entities, attributes, and relationships in a hierarchical structure providing a common language across roles

extractedEntities.name: Unified Data Model
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of creating a standardized, structured way to link diverse datasets from fragmented, siloed sources for mission-critical environments

extractedEntities.name: Public Sector Data Integration
extractedEntities.description: Use case where government teams integrate fragmented, siloed data from countless sources for national security, public safety, and disaster response

keyInsights: The Ontology provides a common language ensuring consistency and accessibility regardless of technical expertise, serving data scientists and decision-makers alike
The Ontology is positioned as the heart of all Palantir platforms (not just Foundry), serving as the unifying framework across the entire product ecosystem
Public sector teams face uniquely challenging data integration problems with fragmented, siloed data where mission success depends on rapid, informed action
The Ontology's value is in surfacing relevant information and connecting dots between datasets, making analysis intuitive rather than requiring technical query skills

title: Polymorphic Interface with Unique Primary Keys - Best Practice?

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: Ontology abstraction enabling polymorphic modeling where multiple object types share common properties like task_id, status, and priority through interface implementation

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Concrete Ontology type (e.g., Incident, Case, Problem) that implements an interface to share common properties

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Relationship mechanism used to connect objects via interface-level references through toInterfaceRid for polymorphic linking

extractedEntities.name: Primary Key
extractedEntities.description: Unique identifier for object instances enforced locally per object type, not globally across the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Interface Polymorphism
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of using a shared interface (e.g., Task) across multiple object types to enable polymorphic references and links from other object types like SLA

extractedEntities.name: toInterfaceRid
extractedEntities.description: Mechanism for creating polymorphic references through interface RIDs, enabling a single link type (e.g., from SLA) to reference any implementing object type

extractedEntities.name: Local Primary Key Enforcement
extractedEntities.description: Foundry enforces primary key uniqueness only within a single object type, meaning the same key value can exist across multiple implementing types, breaking interface-level uniqueness assumptions

keyInsights: Foundry enforces primary key uniqueness only locally per object type, not globally across implementing types, which creates a challenge for interface-level polymorphic references
The toInterfaceRid mechanism enables polymorphic linking from a single object type (e.g., SLA) to any object type implementing a shared interface (e.g., Task)
The community-reported challenge of global uniqueness across interface implementations suggests this is an unresolved design tension in Ontology's polymorphism model
Real-world polymorphic modeling (Incident, Case, Problem sharing a Task interface) requires careful primary key design to avoid collisions across implementing types

title: Introducing object type groups in the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Object Type Group
extractedEntities.description: Fully-featured Ontology primitive replacing legacy tag-based groups, allowing builders to classify object types for improved searchability and legibility of complex ontologies

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element that can be classified with groups for organizational purposes

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application with a new Groups menu in the left-hand navigation bar for viewing, modifying, searching, and filtering object type groups

extractedEntities.name: Object Explorer
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application whose Overview page uses groups to categorize object types for user discovery

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Foundry application that previously displayed legacy groups, which were hidden for non-migrated groups after May 2024

extractedEntities.name: Legacy Group Migration
extractedEntities.description: Migration process from tag-based groups to first-class ontology primitives, with automatic migration for most groups and manual Upgrade Assistant intervention for partially visible groups

extractedEntities.name: Group Visibility
extractedEntities.description: All groups are now discoverable to any user that can view the Ontology, aligning with other ontology primitives for governance transparency

keyInsights: Object type groups graduated from tag-based metadata to a fully-featured Ontology primitive, reflecting Palantir's pattern of promoting successful patterns into first-class features
The visibility change (all groups discoverable to ontology viewers) represents a deliberate governance design decision favoring transparency over restrictive access control
Legacy groups that could not be safely auto-migrated were hidden from operational applications but preserved as type class metadata for backward compatibility
Community question about including many-to-many links in groups suggests users want organizational primitives beyond just object type classification

title: List Object Types - API Reference - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: List Object Types API V1
extractedEntities.description: REST API endpoint (GET /api/v1/ontologies/{ontologyRid}/objectTypes) that returns paginated object type definitions with metadata including API names, properties, primary keys, and descriptions

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element returned by the API with apiName, description, primaryKey array, properties map, and RID

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Object type attribute represented in API responses with a baseType field indicating the data type (String, Integer, Date, etc.)

extractedEntities.name: Primary Key
extractedEntities.description: Array of property names uniquely identifying instances of an object type, represented as a string array in V1 API responses

extractedEntities.name: Ontology RID
extractedEntities.description: Unique Resource Identifier for an Ontology instance, used as a path parameter for API access and discoverable via the List Ontologies endpoint

extractedEntities.name: Object Type RID
extractedEntities.description: Unique resource identifier for an object type (e.g., ri.ontology.main.object-type.401ac022-89eb-4591-8b7e-0a912b9efb44)

extractedEntities.name: OAuth Scope api:ontologies-read
extractedEntities.description: Required OAuth2 operation scope for third-party applications accessing the List Object Types endpoint

extractedEntities.name: Pagination Token
extractedEntities.description: Token-based pagination mechanism using pageToken and nextPageToken fields with configurable page sizes defaulting to 500

keyInsights: V1 API represents object type properties with a simple baseType string (Integer, String, Date) while the property map is keyed by property API names
Primary keys are represented as string arrays in V1 (supporting composite keys), with each entry being a property API name
Object types have unique RIDs following the pattern ri.ontology.main.object-type.{uuid}, providing stable identifiers independent of API names
The API uses token-based pagination with a default page size of 500 and guarantees at least one result per page when more results are available
Third-party OAuth2 applications need the api:ontologies-read scope, indicating a granular permission model for API access

title: Get Object Type - API Reference - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Get Object Type API V2
extractedEntities.description: REST API endpoint (GET /api/v2/ontologies/{ontology}/objectTypes/{objectType}) that retrieves a specific object type by API name with enhanced metadata including display names, status, visibility, and icon

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element with V2 API representation including apiName, displayName, pluralDisplayName, status, description, primaryKey, titleProperty, visibility, icon, properties map, and RID

extractedEntities.name: Object Type Status
extractedEntities.description: Enum representing the release status of an object type with values: ACTIVE, ENDORSED, EXPERIMENTAL, DEPRECATED

extractedEntities.name: Object Type Visibility
extractedEntities.description: Enum representing suggested visibility of an object type with values: NORMAL, PROMINENT, HIDDEN

extractedEntities.name: Property V2
extractedEntities.description: Enhanced property representation in V2 API using a structured dataType object with a type field instead of V1's flat baseType string

extractedEntities.name: Title Property
extractedEntities.description: Designated property on an object type that serves as the display title, a V2-specific metadata field not present in V1

extractedEntities.name: Blueprint Icon
extractedEntities.description: Union type for object type icons, currently consisting only of BlueprintIcon with potential for future icon types

extractedEntities.name: Branch Parameter
extractedEntities.description: Experimental V2 API feature allowing object type definitions to be loaded from a specific Foundry branch rather than the default branch

extractedEntities.name: OAuth Scope api:ontologies-read
extractedEntities.description: Required OAuth2 operation scope for third-party applications accessing the Get Object Type V2 endpoint

keyInsights: V2 API introduces significant metadata enhancements over V1: displayName, pluralDisplayName, status enum (ACTIVE/ENDORSED/EXPERIMENTAL/DEPRECATED), visibility enum (NORMAL/PROMINENT/HIDDEN), titleProperty, and icon
V2 represents properties with a structured dataType object containing a type field, replacing V1's flat baseType string for richer type information
The primaryKey field changed from an array of strings in V1 to a single string in V2, suggesting V2 may have simplified composite key representation
The status enum (ACTIVE, ENDORSED, EXPERIMENTAL, DEPRECATED) provides a lifecycle model for object types, enabling governance of ontology evolution
Experimental branch support in V2 allows loading ontology definitions from non-default branches, suggesting versioned ontology development workflows
